title: Lesson 385
transcript: Okay, let's talk briefly about SASE, the Secure Access Service Edge, SASE. This is a term that was coined by Gartner in 2019, I think. And they're hailing it as the future of wide area networks. So what is SASE? SASE is basically combining security and wide area network capabilities into a single cloud-based service. The basic idea is this. As companies become more distributed, as more people are working from home, there's less and less of this reliance of where everyone's in a single office building, in a single location. And because everything's becoming so much more distributed, we should probably also distribute our security controls. So our firewall, our DLP system, our VPN, all this sort of stuff. If we keep all of this in one central office, it can provide for a lot more latency and it can be a lot more difficult to manage this for all of our remote workers and remote offices and things like that. So the whole idea behind SASE is to basically get these security controls as close to users as possible. So SASE is also sort of combined with edge computing. Remember, edge computing is the idea of getting computational power as close to the user as possible. So the whole idea of SASE is that you basically move these services out onto your edge network, your VPN, your firewall services, your DLP systems, all these various security controls. You move them out onto the network edge as close to users as possible, and you kind of combine it with your WAN technologies, your wide area networks. And the basic idea is to just distribute these technologies basically as a cloud service, get them as close to the user as possible, physically as close to the user as possible. And it's going to make for higher performance, lower latency for them, et cetera. So that's the idea of SASE.
